metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Food Studies 10 examines food systems, nutrition science, food security, and the social and environmental dimensions of food. Students analyze food production, supply chains, and consumption patterns while developing critical thinking about food equity, sustainability, and culture. Through research and practical projects, they explore how food connects to health, environment, and social justice.
metadata.general_description: Elective course focused on food systems, nutrition science, food security, and sustainability.

big_ideas.statement: Food systems are complex networks that connect production, distribution, consumption, and waste across global and local scales.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn to trace food from farm to plate, understanding how agricultural practices, transportation, processing, and retail affect the food available to them. They explore both industrial and alternative food systems.

big_ideas.statement: Nutrition science provides tools for understanding how food affects health, but is shaped by culture, access, and socioeconomic factors.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students develop nutritional literacy while recognizing that food choices are influenced by culture, tradition, availability, and economics. Food equity and access are justice issues, not just individual choices.

big_ideas.statement: Food production has significant environmental consequences that affect ecosystems, climate, and the ability of future generations to be fed.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students explore agriculture's impact on soil, water, biodiversity, and climate. They learn about sustainable practices and the connections between food choices and environmental health.

big_ideas.statement: Food security and food justice require understanding and addressing inequality, culture, and the right to food.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students examine why some people lack access to nutritious food, explore cultural dimensions of food, and learn about community responses to food insecurity. They understand food as a human right.

competencies.raw: Design solutions to food security and access challenges
i_can_statements: I can identify food security issues in communities
I can research and design programs to address food insecurity
I can plan nutritious, affordable meals for various populations
I can evaluate the effectiveness of food security solutions

competencies.raw: Design sustainable food systems and production approaches
i_can_statements: I can research sustainable agricultural practices
I can design reduced-impact food systems
I can plan gardens, farms, or food production projects
I can evaluate environmental impacts of food choices

competencies.raw: Create nutrition and health-focused food plans
i_can_statements: I can apply nutrition science to design healthy diets
I can create culturally appropriate and inclusive meal plans
I can design nutrition programs for specific populations
I can address dietary needs and food allergies in planning

competencies.raw: Design food education and advocacy initiatives
i_can_statements: I can create educational materials about food and nutrition
I can develop campaigns promoting food justice
I can design programs that celebrate food cultures
I can advocate for systemic food system change

competencies.raw: Conduct research on food systems and nutrition
i_can_statements: I can analyze food supply chains and production systems
I can research nutritional content and claims critically
I can evaluate sources and data about food and health
I can synthesize research findings into actionable insights
I can read and interpret nutrition labels and claims
I can assess the nutritional value of foods
I can identify misleading food marketing
I can make informed nutritional choices
I can grow food in gardens or other production systems
I can prepare meals from whole, unprocessed ingredients
I can reduce food waste through planning and composting
I can support local and sustainable food systems
I can explain food system concepts to others
I can advocate for food justice and sustainability
I can present research and evidence about food topics
I can build coalitions around food system change

competencies.raw: Use technology to analyze food systems and data
i_can_statements: I can use digital tools to research and map food systems
I can analyze nutrition data and food production statistics
I can use databases and resources to track food sources
I can visualize and present data about food systems
I can use technology in food production and gardening
I can understand precision agriculture and technology
I can explore food processing and preservation technologies
I can evaluate new food technologies critically

competencies.raw: Use digital tools for food planning and education
i_can_statements: I can use recipe and meal planning apps
I can calculate nutritional values using digital tools
I can create educational materials about food
I can share information about food systems online

competencies.raw: Understand technology's role in food futures
i_can_statements: I can explore emerging food technologies (vertical farming, lab-grown meat, etc.)
I can evaluate technology solutions to food challenges
I can understand e-commerce and food delivery systems
I can consider ethical implications of food technology

areas.name: Food Production and Supply Systems
topics: Global and local food production systems

areas.name: Nutrition Science and Health
topics: Dietary guidelines and healthy eating patterns
Nutrition claims and critical evaluation of food marketing

areas.name: Environmental and Sustainability Dimensions
topics: Environmental impacts of agriculture and food production
Food waste and circular economy approaches
Food miles and local food systems

areas.name: Food Security, Justice, and Culture
topics: Food insecurity and hunger causes and solutions
Food equity and access across populations
Food sovereignty and Indigenous food systems
